item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Have to downvote due to totally inadequate descriptions of military operations, making me unable to suspend my disbelief. There is very little hope for any other nation to launch any sort of amphibious invasion of American soil without liberal use of nuclear weapons. That the war lasted for so long implies a strangely half-hearted deployment of strategic nuclear weapons - when the deliberate launching of one would inevitably set off a counter-strike of greater magnitude. Mutually assured destruction.

The war never happened because the war as it's described in the document is too silly to have existed. Kauai can only be taken if all US air and naval presence from Hawaii and surrounding regions have been destroyed. This implies the annihilation of the US Pacific Fleet. Even if our 2020's China were mobilized for war, they still have no experience nor even doctrine for conducting naval operations of that scale. China's military today isn't even geared towards Pacific dominance. Its main concern is projecting regional power towards Taiwan and India. Its naval stance is strikingly defensive. Our PRC has the largest coastal defense force in the world, bristling with battery upon battery of anti-ship missiles. It is defensive because it doesn't have the political and financial resources to be offensive. Because it is the weaker military. A poorer 2000's China simply cannot hope to do anything about Hawaii, much less the US mainland.

The only real offensive capabilities China has against the US would be its strategic nuclear arsenal. It is tiny when compared to its American counterpart. The moment China uses one on the United States, to attack a military installation, then the Americans will use a liberal definition of the term 'military installation' and launch a significantly more devastating counter-attack. If the PRC decides to strike at a population center, then the US is sure to nuke Beijing, Shanghai, Nanking and everything else.

A total war with nukes lasting this long between two combatant nations of vastly differing capabilities is simply not possible. Your military science is unsound.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this article, the idea feels ripe for content. The ending line feels slightly undeveloped though. However, despite that, it is still a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The audio logs combined with the twist made this a very fun read!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has an incredible amount of potential and I absolutely love the concept. Unfortunately, the ending falls flat, destroys what was previously established, and takes the whole piece down with it. In retrospect, a lot of the article is quite mundane, especially in terms of how the war was fought and the progression of the events. I feel a lot could be done here, but this just isn't it.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is 1 aspect to this that kills my suspension of disbelief: why would the US deliver a nuclear payload using a bomber, in the modern era? An ICBM can deliver a nuke to a target in minutes flat, and is virtually impossible to counter- perhaps the unspecified nuclear strike over America nullified some launch sites, but there are dozens of them, across the US. I just don't get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, in my opinion that adds to the mystery and appeal of it. Sometimes leaving things to the imagination is far better than explaining everything

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending appears thought-provoking but collapses after a few moments of thought, even with your attempts at lampshading. Of course people wouldn’t realize that the Iraq War was happening if there was nobody reporting it. The containment procedures don’t go nearly deep enough either — how is the media being silenced? What kind of political pressure is being employed to ensure that uninvolved countries keep silent? Most importantly, why would the Foundation want to keep this war a secret? These questions aren’t answered or even touched upon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I said in chat — this is very impressive. It's difficult enough to pull off a multi-media article, especially one that is convincing. There is the fact that this was written and compiled, start to finish, in an accelerated time frame, and that requires a lot of talent. Genuinely one of the most technically impressive articles I've seen since SCP-5050, and before that, since Kate McTiriss graced the site (and that's no exaggeration).

I do hope that readers have a chance to check this one out in the sea of other articles in this contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, here it is—my Jam 2020 Explosions entry.

I challenged myself a little extra for this: The theme was explosions, so I did everything I could to make an explosion-related SCP without using the word "explosion" one single time.

I would like to thank the critters who gave me great feedback, the friend who recorded brand-spanking-new audio for me in under 48 hours, and the community for being, y'know, awesome.

Edit: Been asked a couple times: My friend voiced the first audio clip; I provided all 3 voices for the second audio clip. I then did all the audio editing, including recreating real aviation cockpit alert sounds.

License Disclosures
close
  • "Mappo 12" is derived from Open Streetmap, copyrighted by OpenStreetMap Contributors,with data made available under the Open Database License, and the graphical tile licensed for use under CC-BY-SA.
  • Both recordings and the "Corrupted Photo" are original works by me, and are licensed under CC-BY-SA 3.0.
